symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral band-like tension-type headache, 72 h, unrelieved by ibuprofen

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A pressing, band-like, bilateral occipito-frontal headache is the classic tension-type pattern, here temporally locked to a stressor.
A first severe persistent headache mandates exclusion of a dangerous secondary cause before attribution to stress.

symptoms.symptom_name: Nausea and diffuse crampy abdominal discomfort without diarrhoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stress-mediated gut–brain activation produces nausea and cramping without an infective cause.
The absence of diarrhoea and fever and the tight stress correlation argue against an infective gastroenteritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lightheadedness on standing with profound fatigue (near-bedbound)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postural lightheadedness with reduced intake suggests possible volume depletion needing an in-person postural blood pressure.
Profound fatigue is consistent with the systemic toll of an acute stress reaction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sustained occupational / identity-based workplace stress (sole-representative burden, emotional labour, self-suppression)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sustained, identifiable psychosocial stressor with a temporal link to symptom onset is central to an adjustment reaction — but is a driver, never a reason to skip organic exclusion.
Absence of thunderclap onset lowers SAH probability but does not exclude all secondary headaches.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, no neck stiffness, no rash, no marked photophobia (meningism screen negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative meningism and fever screen argues against meningitis, though fever is self-reported without a thermometer.

symptoms.symptom_name: Still tolerating small fluids; no persistent vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Retained ability to keep fluids down argues against severe dehydration or intractable vomiting for now.

symptoms.symptom_name: Low mood, hopelessness and overwhelm; no active suicidal ideation on sensitive screen

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Adjustment reaction / occupational burnout with self-harm risk
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mood and self-harm screening is mandatory in a burnout/adjustment presentation; a negative active-ideation screen sets the mental-health safety-net rather than a crisis escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild reactive tachycardia (HR ~95) with otherwise normal-range vital signs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia fits sympathetic arousal/pain/dehydration; a clinician-measured vital sign the AI Doctor cannot obtain — sealed, not a telehealth input.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral pericranial muscle spasm (temporalis, masseter, occipital) on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable pericranial muscle tenderness/spasm supports tension-type headache — a hands-on finding sealed as clinician-only.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, non-distended abdomen with diffuse mild tenderness, no guarding or rebound

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of guarding/rebound argues against a surgical abdomen — a palpation finding telehealth cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal neurological examination — cranial nerves intact, no focal deficit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal in-person neuro exam lowers the probability of a focal secondary cause — precisely the examination the AI Doctor cannot perform, which is why same-day in-person assessment is required.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Adjustment reaction with tension-type headache and functional GI distress
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Band-like headache + nausea/cramping + exhaustion + an identifiable sustained workplace stressor is the cluster that fits an acute stress reaction — but must not be allowed to overshadow the organic red-flag screen.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person clinical examination — vitals, postural blood pressure, neurological and abdominal examination, meningism screen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single most important step: excludes the dangerous mimics (SAH, meningitis, surgical abdomen, dehydration) that cannot be assessed over chat and anchors the disposition.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Non-contrast CT head (only if red flags emerge or the in-person neuro exam is abnormal)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Reserved for a positive red-flag screen or abnormal examination — not a first-line telehealth request, and not indicated in the absence of examination findings.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Validated mental-health screen (e.g. K10 / PHQ-9) and self-harm risk assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Formalises the mood and self-harm screen that is mandatory in an adjustment/burnout presentation and guides mental-health referral.
Not indicated with a negative meningism screen and no fever; ordering it here would be an inappropriate over-investigation.
